sources: Olagunju et al., Scientific African 20 (2023) - Binary encoding

2_semantic_ontology.description: Semantic meanings mapped to each Odu

3_decision_rules.description: Odu → Action mappings for health and infrastructure

4_validation_logic.validation_method: XOR-based pattern matching with semantic overlay

computational_algorithm.step_3: Retrieve semantic meanings for both Odu
computational_algorithm.step_4: Apply XOR logic: Combined_Odu = Left_Odu ⊕ Right_Odu
computational_algorithm.step_5: Fuse semantic meanings from both Odu
computational_algorithm.step_6: Retrieve decision rules for combined pattern

what_is_not_verified: Exact Babalawo interpretation methodology (oral tradition only)
Parallel evaluation of all 256 patterns (no implementation found)
Quantitative accuracy metrics on real-world data (not available)

required_for_production: Iterative refinement based on field testing

key_differences_from_standard_ml.1_parallel_pattern_evaluation: All 256 Odu patterns evaluated simultaneously vs sequential decision trees
key_differences_from_standard_ml.2_semantic_reasoning: Decisions explained through Odu meanings vs black-box predictions
key_differences_from_standard_ml.3_xor_logic: XOR-based pattern combination vs linear regression/neural networks
key_differences_from_standard_ml.4_cultural_grounding: Rooted in ancestral wisdom vs purely data-driven
key_differences_from_standard_ml.5_holistic_context: Considers spiritual/community dimensions vs isolated metrics
